mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and impending recession, causing investors to sell equities and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in over 65% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate robust consumer demand and construction activity, supporting corporate earnings and positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate revenues and positive short-term equity returns during growth phases.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative or flat when INDPRO trends upward, yielding a Rank IC below 0.01 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index values above 230 indicate entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally with positive 20-day returns in over 70% of observations when CPIAUCSL remains above 230.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates a strengthening labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, driving positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS momentum drops below 0.03 over rolling 3-year periods, or 60-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing real GDP growth and rising real personal consumption expenditures signals unsustainable consumer leverage, triggering mean reversion and negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence condition fails to predict negative 20-day returns, with positive outcomes occurring in over 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production coinciding with falling inflation indicates a supply-side recovery that boosts profit margins without triggering rate hikes, generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in over 55% of instances when industrial production rises alongside declining CPIAUCSL.
